Class IuDataSource

java.lang.Object
edu.iu.jdbc.pool.IuDataSource
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable, Wrapper, CommonDataSource, DataSource
public class IuDataSource extends Object implements DataSource, AutoCloseable
JTA-aware DataSource backed by an IuConnectionPool.

Accepts an IuDataSourceIntegration for hooking into the application's transaction manager and an IuConnectionPoolConfiguration for tuning pool behavior. The pool is created on construction and closed when close() is called; callers are responsible for lifecycle management, typically via a try-with-resources block or a dependency-injection container.

Connection dispatch

  • No active JTA transaction – getConnection() checks a connection out of the pool and returns it directly. The caller is responsible for closing the connection, which returns it to the pool.
  • Active JTA transaction (non-XA) – the first call to getConnection() within a transaction checks out a connection, disables auto-commit, sets isolation to Connection.TRANSACTION_SERIALIZABLE, and registers a JTA Synchronization that commits on beforeCompletion and rolls back on afterCompletion if the transaction did not commit. Subsequent calls within the same transaction return the same connection wrapped in a proxy that silently ignores Connection.close(), deferring the actual close to the synchronization callback.
  • Active JTA transaction (XA-capable) – when the pooled connection implements XAConnection, its XAResource is enlisted with the transaction manager. Commit and rollback are driven by the transaction manager; the connection is closed in afterCompletion.

Unsupported operations

getConnection(String, String), setLogWriter(PrintWriter), setLoginTimeout(int), and unwrap(Class) all throw SQLFeatureNotSupportedException.
